question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $94,000 on November 19?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price Analysis 2025: Trends & Expert Forecasts
evidences.content: The market sentiment for Bitcoin in late 2025 appears cautiously optimistic, with technical indicators suggesting a potential for growth, though short-term fluctuations are to be expected. The influence of institutional investment and broader economic conditions will likely be key drivers. [...] ### Average Trading Price Expectations Beyond the extremes, the average trading price expectation from the Bitcoin price analysis 2025 report hovers around $125,444.50. This middle-ground estimate attempts to balance the optimistic outlook with the inherent volatility of the crypto market. Several factors, including the ongoing adoption of spot Bitcoin ETFs and the anticipation surrounding future halving events, are expected to play a major role in shaping this average. ### Monthly Price Fluctuations [...] ### Projected Minimum and Maximum Values Forecasting exact price points is challenging, but many analyses within the Bitcoin price analysis 2025 outlook point toward significant upward potential. For 2025, some projections place the minimum expected value around $113,245.54, with the maximum potentially reaching $119,345.02. These figures are based on various technical indicators and m...

evidences.title: Bitcoin ETF Inflows For 2025 Now Outpace 2024, Data Shows
evidences.content: From the chart, it’s apparent that 2025 cumulative net inflows stand at $14.8381 billion right now. At the same phase of the year in 2024, they amounted to $14.8278 billion. The spot ETF demand had been sideways at this point of the time last year, so it’s possible that if inflows continue at the current pace, 2025 will gain some distance. That said, it only remains to be seen how long demand can keep up, as in 2024, inflows saw an acceleration toward the end of the year as interest flooded into the market alongside the surge beyond $100,000. In some other news, US Ethereum spot ETFs have been seeing a boom of their own recently, as the netflow has remained green for many weeks now. evidences.title: Crypto Market Trends: Bitcoin Price Forecasts and the Rise of ...
evidences.content: ## Future Outlook ### Predictions for 2025 and Beyond The future outlook for Bitcoin and the cryptocurrency market remains optimistic, with several analysts predicting significant price appreciation. According to a report by Fool.com, the price of Bitcoin could reach nearly $150,000 by 2025 in a base case scenario, driven by continued institutional investment and the approval of Bitcoin ETFs. In a bull case scenario, the price could potentially reach $1 million or more. Bernstein’s report predicts that Bitcoin will reach $200,000 in 2025, $500,000 by 2029, and $1 million by 2033, driven by further institutional adoption and ETF inflows. ### Factors Influencing Future Trends Several factors will influence future trends in the cryptocurrency market: [...] A report by broker Bernstein suggests that Bitcoin will reach a cycle high of $200,000 in 2025, $500,000 by 2029, and $1 million by 2033. evidences.title: Assets Options Volume & OI - BTC - Laevitas
evidences.content: The max pain price is calculated by evaluating the total open interest at each strike and finding the strike price where the combined option premiums are minimized for option writers. The concept of max pain suggests that as expiration approaches, the asset's price may move toward this level because it represents a point of maximum financial loss for option buyers and minimum payout for option sellers. [...] This phenomenon, known as "pinning," can cause the asset price to stabilize around the max pain level, especially when there is high open interest or significant gamma (sensitivity of delta to the underlying asset's price) at a particular strike price. As expiration nears, the price may be "pinned" around the max pain level due to the hedging activities of options market participants, potentially reducing price volatility. [...] BTC DERIBIT Open Interest By Strike This chart displays open interest by strike for various expirations. It shows the number of call and put options contracts for each strike price, giving an overview of the distribution of open interest across different price levels. The chart also allows users to see the "max pain" point for a specific expiration w...

evidences.title: Kline Candlestick Data | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: Binance Logo Binance Logo # Kline/Candlestick Data ## API Description​ Kline/candlestick bars for a symbol. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. Kline/Candlestick chart intervals: m -> minutes; h -> hours; d -> days; w -> weeks; M -> months ## HTTP Request​ GET `/dapi/v1/klines` `/dapi/v1/klines` ## Request Weight​ based on parameter `LIMIT` `LIMIT` | LIMIT | weight | --- | | [1,100) | 1 | | [100, 500) | 2 | | [500, 1000] | 5 | 1000 | 10 ## Request Parameters​ | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | After CM migration, accepts both CM and UM symbols. | | interval | ENUM | YES | | | startTime | LONG | NO | | | endTime | LONG | NO | | | limit | INT | NO | Default 500; max 1500. | [...] `startTime` `endTime` `startTime` `endTime` `limit` `endTime` `startTime` `endTime` `endTime` `startTime` `startTime` `endTime` `endTime` `endTime` `startTime` ## Response Example​ `[ [ 1591258320000, // Open time "9640.7", // Open "9642.4", // High "9640.6", // Low "9642.0", // Close (or latest price) "206", // Volume 1591258379999, // Close time "2.13660389", // Base asset volume 48, // Number of trades "119", // Taker buy volume "1.23424865", //...

evidences.title: Market Data Endpoints — python-binance 0.2.0 documentation
evidences.content: Fetch klines for any date range and interval ``` # fetch 1 minute klines for the last day up until now klines = client. get_historical_klines("BNBBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, "1 day ago UTC") # fetch 30 minute klines for the last month of 2017 klines = client. get_historical_klines("ETHBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_30MINUTE,"1 Dec, 2017","1 Jan, 2018") # fetch weekly klines since it listed klines = client. get_historical_klines("NEOBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1WEEK,"1 Jan, 2017") ``` ## Get Historical Kline/Candlesticks using a generator Fetch klines using a generator ``` for kline in client. get_historical_klines_generator("BNBBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, "1 day ago UTC"): print(kline) # do something with the kline ``` ## Get average price for a symbol [...] ``` agg_trades = client. aggregate_trade_iter(symbol = 'ETHBTC', start_str = '30 minutes ago UTC') # iterate over the trade iterator for trade in agg_trades: print(trade) # do something with the trade data # convert the iterator to a list# note: generators can only be iterated over once so we need to call it again agg_trades = client. aggregate_trade_iter(symbol = 'ETHBTC', '30 minutes ago UTC') agg_trad...

evidences.title: Bitcoin News: BTC's $12B Quarterly Options Settlement Looks Balanced and Might Be Subdued, Deribit Says
evidences.content: Quarterly expiries of such massive magnitudes are known to breed market volatility, but that may not be the case this time, going by the continued decline in the bitcoin 30-day implied volatility index (DVOL). The index has dropped from an annualized 62% to 48% in the weeks leading up to the expiry, suggesting subdued volatility expectations. Similar conclusions can be drawn from the annualized perpetual futures basis of around 5% on the exchange, signalling a calmer funding environment. "Despite the size of the expiry, the overall setup—low DVOL, moderate basis, and balanced options positioning—points to a relatively subdued expiry unless external catalysts emerge," Luuk Strijers, CEO of Deribit, told CoinDesk. Image 2: Deribit: BTC's quarterly options expiry. (Deribit Metrics) [...] Bitcoin BTC$64,025.03 options worth billions of dollars will expire on Deribit on Friday. The impending settlement, though big, may not yield significant market volatility, the exchange told CoinDesk. More than 139,000 BTC option contracts, worth $12.13 billion, representing nearly 45% of the total active BTC contracts across all expiries, are due for settlement this Friday, according to data sourc...

evidences.title: Treasury Curve Forecast | econforecasting.com
evidences.content: #### YIELD CURVE FORECASTS #### VIEW DATA #### DESCRIPTION Forecasts shown on this page are from our market consensus forecast of Treasury yields. It is generated primarily using yield data, futures market prices, and survey data, using minimal econometric assumptions. Forecasts from the model can be interpreted as the median expectation of market participants. The Treasury yield component of the forecast is generated using three seperate component forecasts: Full methodology can be found in the model documentation. While the model prioritizes consistency with market expectations over forecast accuracy, its forecast accuracy has historically exceeded that of survey and agency-based forecasts due to its higher update frequency and timeliness. Prior forecast values are available below. [...] The model is updated daily at 9:35 ET using prior-day closing values. Key inputs include the prior trading day's futures prices (as of a 3pm ET settlement time); the prior business day's Treasury par yield curves data, obtained from the U.S. Treasury; and the prior business day's effective fed funds rate prices, obtained from the New York Fed. ##### CONTACT
